content_en:
Allah combined these two types of du’ as in numerous verses in the Quran, the simplest of them being the oft-recited verse:

So this verse clearly shows the important relationship between du'‘a al-‘ibadah, and dua al-mas’alah, and the wise person is he , who understands and acts upon both of these types of du‘a in his life.

The second category of people are those that have left both types of du‘, so they neither worship Allah, nor seek His help or aid in any matter. So in this category fall the people that worship false deities, believing these deities to be worthy of their du’ a al-' ibadah and du‘ a al-mas’alah. They never turn to Allah, and thus have lost this world and the Hereafter.

38 seerah al-Fatihah, 5.

Also, those that have rejected religion entirely, such as the communists, atheists and agnostics, fall into this category as well. To such people, the only way to achieve any goal is through physical means, for in their rejection of a god they have implicitly worshipped creation. And these are the worst of mankind.

The third category are those people that practice du’ a al-' ibadah, but ignore du’ a al-mas’alah. Such thinking can only come from an ignorant person, or one who is deviated in his beliefs.

As for the ignorant Muslim — and unfortunately how common they are in our times — then he forgets that the real way and sure path to achieve his goal is by making dz’ a to Allah. He becomes so ‘ caught up in this world that, although he might pray and fast and give zakah, he forgets the spiritual aspect of Islam, and the beauty of the Names and Attributes of Allah. To such a person, Islam becomes a series of mundane acts, devoid of any meaning, performed at regular intervals. Such a person needs to re-evaluate his concept of Islam, and even of life, in order that he can correct his misunderstanding. He must ponder over the meanings of the Names and Attributes of Allah, and realise the ultimate Power and Knowledge of the Creator, and only then will he be able to see the error of his ways. He must realise that every act he does, whether it is related to this world or the Hereafter, must be done with the help and aid of Allah, for if Allah does not help him in achieving what he desires, he will never be able to achieve it.

As for the person who actually believes that it is incorrect to use du‘ a al-mas’alah, and instead restricts himself to du’ a al-' ibadah, then such a person without a doubt has committed a grave mistake. These people are only found in deviated sects — sects that have perverted the meanings of the Qur’an and Sunnah in order to justify their positions. Examples of this are the extreme Safis, for many of them believe that it is actually a sin to ask Allah for anything! Basing this beliefon a few fabricated ahadith,” they claim that a person

must always be content with what Allah decrees for him, and therefore should not pray for a change in his situation! The falseness of this logic is in their understanding of ‘contentment,’ for to be content with Allah means that one should not curse the Divine Decree, or be angry at Allah for what has occurred. It does not mean that one must be happy and satisfied at every incident that occurs to one, particularly if it is related to a sin or an evil pertaining to one’s religion. In addition, we find that all Allah’s prophets, without exception, made dua to Allah for all matters. Therefore, this understanding of the extreme Sifis is not in accordance with the understanding of the prophets.

The fourth, and final, category are those people that ignore du’ a al-' ibadah, and only practice du‘ a al-mas’alah. Such people believe in Allah, but follow their own selfish desires, and strive in order to satisfy their every whim. So they leave worship of Allah, but they realise that Allah is the Controller of all affairs, and, therefore, ask Him for their selfish needs.

The primary example of this is Iblis, Satan himself, may Allah’s eternal curse be upon him. For when he was expelled from Paradise due to his arrogance, he actually made a dw‘ a to Allah, du’ a almas'alah — to allow him to live until the Day of Judgement. And Allah responded to his du’ @ and granted him his wish, even though Iblis knew full well that this time that he was granted would only be used to mislead others, out of his spite and jealousy. So Iblis left du‘a alibadah out of arrogance, but was forced to use du'aal- . mas‘alah out of greed.

Likewise, all those who have preferred the life of this world over the Hereafter have fallen into the same mistake as Iblis.

Allah states in the Quran:

ey Nl calles ees ogo iis

And there are those amongst mankind who say, ‘O Allah! Give us in this life,’ and they will have no share of the Hereafter. And there are those who say, ‘O Allah! Give us good in this

life, and good in the Hereafter, and save us from the Fire of Hell!’ These shall have a share of what they earned, and Allah is swift in Reckoning.”

So these are the four categories of people when it comes to practicing both categories of dua.

4. With Respect to What is Asked

The actual dw‘ @ itself can be categorised in many different ways, depending on which perspective one looks from.

So, it is possible to divide what is asked for into two categories: matters pertaining to religion, and matters pertaining to the world. Examples of the first type are to ask for increased faith, or an increase in good deeds, or forgiveness for one’s sins. Examples of the second category are to ask for an increase in money, or to be cured from a disease, or to be granted more children.

The true Muslim asks Ailah from both of these categories, realising that the good pertaining to this world is in reality a means of attaining the good in the Hereafter. So, an increase in wealth, children, and health is in fact a means of coming closer to Allah by obeying His commandments and sacrificing in His way. On the other hand, ignorant and deviated Muslims will only ask Allah for one of these two categories, ignoring the other.

Another way to categorise what is asked for is by examining the benefit or harm it causes. So, it is possible to state that the entire du'@ of all of creation centres around four pillars:

Firstly: good that exists. For example, one might be in a state of good health, or have great wealth. So dua is made that this state lasts, and is not taken away.

Secondly: good that is desired. For example, a person does not have good health, but wishes for it. This is asked for by means of du ‘a.

Thirdly: evil that exists. For example, a person might be sick, or suffer from poverty. So dua is made that this situation be changed.

Fourthly: evil that does not exist. For example, a person might fear a certain disease, or another trial. So du‘ @ is made that this evil never occurs, and is averted.

All of these four types of du‘ @ are combined in the comprehensive du ‘a that is found in the last verses of seerah Al-" Imran:

Beet 47 BA CR Dee ge

a7 eR wt ke tee eek Gee gace CLARY A PIAA

Our Lord! Forgive us our sins, and remit us from our evil deeds, and cause us to dic in a state of righteousness. Our Lord! Grant us what you promised us through Your messengers, and do

not disgrace us on the Day of Judgement, for You never break Your promise.

So the phrase, ‘Forgive us our sins, and remit us from our evil deeds’ is a du‘ a that an existing evil be removed. And the phrase, ‘and cause us to die in a state of righteousness’, is a du‘ @ asking that an existing good, that of the presence of iman, continue and not be taken away until death. And the phrase, ‘Grant us what you promised us through Your messengers’, is a dua for a good that does not yet exist to be given. Lastly, the phrase, ‘do not disgrace us on the Day of Judgement’ ,is a du‘ @ to avert an evil that does not exist.

4! Sarah AL‘ Imran, 193-194.

THE EXCELLENCE AND BENEFITS OF Du A

This is due to the fact that du'@ is a means of showing one’s poverty and incapability to Allah. It is a manner of humiliating one’s self to Allah and acknowledging the power and capabilities of Allah, all Glory and Praise be to Him. This has already been mentioned in the hadith we quoted wherein du‘ is a part of worship.

Some of the benefits of du’ @ are as follows:

1. Du‘a is the Most Noble of all Acts in the Sight of Allah

This is based on the above hadith: “There is nothing that is more noble in the sight of Allah than du’ a”."* Al-Shawkani’’ commented

” Authentic, narrated by Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, al-Hakim, and others, all of

them from Abt Hurayrah, as has been mentioned in Sahih al-Jami # 5392.

See: al-Du' a, al-Hamad, pp. 16-19.

Authentic, narrated by Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, al-Hakim, and others, all of them from Abt Hurayrah, as has been mentioned in Sahih al-Jami # 5392.

Tuhfat al-Dhakirin, p. 30.

on this hadith by stating: “It has been said that this is the case because of the fact that it shows the Power of Allah, and the incapability of the one making dw‘ a. But it is more correct to say that since du ais worship, and, as confirmed in another hadith, the essence of worship, it is the most noble act because of this position. This because mankind has only been created to worship Allah, as Allah states,
content_id:
Allah menggabungkan dua jenis du'a ini dalam banyak ayat dalam Al-Quran, yang paling sederhana adalah ayat yang sering dibaca:

Maka ayat ini dengan jelas menunjukkan hubungan penting antara du'a al-'ibadah dan du'a al-mas'alah, dan orang yang bijaksana adalah dia yang memahami dan mengamalkan kedua jenis du'a ini dalam kehidupannya.

Kategori kedua adalah orang-orang yang telah meninggalkan kedua jenis du'a, sehingga mereka tidak menyembah Allah, tidak pula memohon pertolongan atau bantuan-Nya dalam urusan apapun. Maka dalam kategori ini termasuk orang-orang yang menyembah sesembahan palsu, meyakini bahwa sesembahan-sesembahan tersebut layak mendapatkan du'a al-'ibadah dan du'a al-mas'alah mereka. Mereka tidak pernah berpaling kepada Allah, dan dengan demikian telah kehilangan dunia dan akhirat.

38 Surah Al-Fatihah, 5.

Juga, mereka yang telah menolak agama sepenuhnya, seperti kaum komunis, ateis, dan agnostik, termasuk dalam kategori ini pula. Bagi orang-orang seperti itu, satu-satunya cara untuk mencapai tujuan adalah melalui cara-cara fisik, karena dalam penolakan mereka terhadap tuhan, mereka secara tidak langsung telah menyembah makhluk. Dan mereka adalah seburuk-buruk manusia.

Kategori ketiga adalah orang-orang yang mengamalkan du'a al-'ibadah, namun mengabaikan du'a al-mas'alah. Pemikiran seperti itu hanya bisa datang dari orang yang bodoh, atau seseorang yang menyimpang dalam keyakinannya.

Adapun Muslim yang jahil — dan betapa banyaknya mereka di zaman kita — maka dia lupa bahwa cara nyata dan jalan pasti untuk mencapai tujuannya adalah dengan berdu'a kepada Allah. Dia begitu terlena dengan dunia sehingga, meskipun dia mungkin shalat, berpuasa, dan membayar zakah, dia melupakan aspek spiritual Islam, dan keindahan Asma dan Sifat Allah. Bagi orang seperti itu, Islam menjadi serangkaian amalan yang membosankan, tanpa makna, dilakukan pada waktu-waktu tertentu. Orang seperti itu perlu mengevaluasi kembali pemahamannya tentang Islam, dan bahkan tentang kehidupan, agar dia dapat memperbaiki kesalahpahamannya. Dia harus merenungkan makna Asma dan Sifat Allah, dan menyadari Kekuasaan dan Ilmu yang mutlak dari Sang Pencipta, dan hanya dengan itulah dia akan mampu melihat kekeliruannya. Dia harus menyadari bahwa setiap perbuatan yang dia lakukan, baik yang berkaitan dengan dunia maupun akhirat, harus dilakukan dengan pertolongan dan bantuan Allah, karena jika Allah tidak menolongnya dalam mencapai apa yang dia inginkan, dia tidak akan pernah mampu meraihnya.

Adapun orang yang benar-benar meyakini bahwa menggunakan du'a al-mas'alah itu tidak benar, dan sebaliknya membatasi dirinya hanya pada du'a al-'ibadah, maka orang seperti itu tanpa keraguan telah melakukan kesalahan yang besar. Orang-orang ini hanya ditemukan dalam kelompok-kelompok yang menyimpang — kelompok yang telah memutarbalikkan makna Al-Quran dan Sunnah demi membenarkan posisi mereka. Contoh dari hal ini adalah kaum Sufi yang ekstrem, karena banyak dari mereka meyakini bahwa memohon sesuatu kepada Allah adalah dosa! Mendasarkan keyakinan ini pada beberapa hadith palsu, mereka mengklaim bahwa seseorang

harus selalu ridha dengan apa yang Allah tetapkan untuknya, dan karena itu tidak perlu berdoa meminta perubahan dalam situasinya! Kekeliruan logika ini terletak pada pemahaman mereka tentang 'ridha', karena ridha kepada Allah berarti seseorang tidak boleh mencaci-maki takdir Ilahi, atau marah kepada Allah atas apa yang telah terjadi. Hal itu tidak berarti bahwa seseorang harus senang dan puas dengan setiap kejadian yang menimpanya, khususnya jika berkaitan dengan suatu dosa atau keburukan yang menyangkut agamanya. Di samping itu, kita mendapati bahwa semua nabi Allah, tanpa pengecualian, berdu'a kepada Allah untuk semua urusan. Oleh karena itu, pemahaman kaum Sufi yang ekstrem ini tidak sesuai dengan pemahaman para nabi.

Kategori keempat, dan terakhir, adalah orang-orang yang mengabaikan du'a al-'ibadah, dan hanya mengamalkan du'a al-mas'alah. Orang-orang seperti itu beriman kepada Allah, namun mengikuti keinginan nafsu mereka sendiri, dan berusaha keras untuk memuaskan setiap kemauan mereka. Maka mereka meninggalkan ibadah kepada Allah, namun mereka menyadari bahwa Allah adalah Pengendali segala urusan, dan karenanya memohon kepada-Nya untuk kebutuhan-kebutuhan egois mereka.

Contoh utama dari hal ini adalah Iblis, Setan itu sendiri, semoga laknat Allah yang abadi menimpanya. Karena ketika dia diusir dari surga akibat kesombongannya, dia justru berdu'a kepada Allah, du'a al-mas'alah — untuk membiarkannya hidup hingga Hari Kiamat. Dan Allah mengabulkan du'anya dan mengabulkan permintaannya, meskipun Iblis mengetahui dengan pasti bahwa waktu yang diberikan kepadanya itu hanya akan digunakan untuk menyesatkan orang lain, karena dengki dan iri hatinya. Maka Iblis meninggalkan du'a al-'ibadah karena kesombongan, namun terpaksa menggunakan du'a al-mas'alah karena keserakahannya.

Demikian pula, semua orang yang lebih mengutamakan kehidupan dunia daripada akhirat telah jatuh ke dalam kesalahan yang sama seperti Iblis.

Allah berfirman dalam Al-Quran:

ey Nl calles ees ogo iis

Dan di antara manusia ada yang berkata, 'Ya Allah! Berikanlah kami kebaikan di dunia ini,' dan mereka tidak mendapat bagian di akhirat. Dan ada pula yang berkata, 'Ya Allah! Berikanlah kami kebaikan di dunia ini, dan kebaikan di akhirat, dan selamatkan kami dari api neraka!' Mereka itulah yang mendapat bagian dari apa yang mereka usahakan, dan Allah Maha Cepat perhitungan-Nya."

Maka itulah empat kategori manusia dalam mengamalkan kedua kategori du'a.

4. Berkenaan dengan Apa yang Dimohonkan

Du'a itu sendiri dapat dikategorikan dalam banyak cara yang berbeda, tergantung dari sudut pandang mana seseorang melihatnya.

Maka, dimungkinkan untuk membagi apa yang dimohonkan ke dalam dua kategori: hal-hal yang berkaitan dengan agama, dan hal-hal yang berkaitan dengan dunia. Contoh jenis pertama adalah memohon bertambahnya iman, atau bertambahnya amal kebaikan, atau ampunan atas dosa-dosa. Contoh kategori kedua adalah memohon bertambahnya harta, atau kesembuhan dari penyakit, atau dikaruniai lebih banyak anak.

Muslim yang sejati memohon kepada Allah dari kedua kategori ini, menyadari bahwa kebaikan yang berkaitan dengan dunia ini pada hakikatnya merupakan sarana untuk meraih kebaikan di akhirat. Maka bertambahnya harta, anak, dan kesehatan pada kenyataannya merupakan sarana untuk mendekatkan diri kepada Allah dengan menaati perintah-perintah-Nya dan berkorban di jalan-Nya. Di sisi lain, Muslim yang jahil dan menyimpang hanya akan memohon kepada Allah dari salah satu dari dua kategori ini, mengabaikan yang lainnya.

Cara lain untuk mengkategorikan apa yang dimohonkan adalah dengan menelaah manfaat atau mudarat yang ditimbulkannya. Maka, dapat dinyatakan bahwa seluruh du'a semua makhluk berpusat pada empat pilar:

Pertama: kebaikan yang sudah ada. Misalnya, seseorang berada dalam kondisi kesehatan yang baik, atau memiliki kekayaan yang besar. Maka du'a dipanjatkan agar kondisi ini berlangsung terus, dan tidak dicabut.

Kedua: kebaikan yang diinginkan. Misalnya, seseorang tidak memiliki kesehatan yang baik, namun menginginkannya. Hal ini dimohonkan melalui du'a.

Ketiga: keburukan yang sudah ada. Misalnya, seseorang sedang sakit, atau menderita kemiskinan. Maka du'a dipanjatkan agar situasi ini berubah.

Keempat: keburukan yang belum ada. Misalnya, seseorang mungkin takut terhadap penyakit tertentu, atau cobaan lainnya. Maka du'a dipanjatkan agar keburukan ini tidak pernah terjadi, dan dapat dihindarkan.

Keempat jenis du'a ini tergabung dalam du'a yang komprehensif yang terdapat pada ayat-ayat terakhir Surah Ali 'Imran:

Beet 47 BA CR Dee ge

a7 eR wt ke tee eek Gee gace CLARY A PIAA

Ya Tuhan kami! Ampunilah dosa-dosa kami, dan hapuskanlah dari kami keburukan-keburukan kami, dan wafatkanlah kami dalam keadaan bersama orang-orang yang berbakti. Ya Tuhan kami! Berikanlah kepada kami apa yang Engkau janjikan kepada kami melalui para rasul-Mu, dan janganlah Engkau hinakan kami pada Hari Kiamat, karena sesungguhnya Engkau tidak pernah mengingkari janji.

Maka ungkapan, 'Ampunilah dosa-dosa kami, dan hapuskanlah dari kami keburukan-keburukan kami' adalah du'a agar keburukan yang sudah ada dihilangkan. Dan ungkapan, 'dan wafatkanlah kami dalam keadaan bersama orang-orang yang berbakti', adalah du'a memohon agar kebaikan yang sudah ada, yaitu kehadiran iman, terus berlangsung dan tidak dicabut hingga kematian. Dan ungkapan, 'Berikanlah kepada kami apa yang Engkau janjikan kepada kami melalui para rasul-Mu', adalah du'a untuk kebaikan yang belum ada agar diberikan. Terakhir, ungkapan, 'janganlah Engkau hinakan kami pada Hari Kiamat', adalah du'a untuk menghindari keburukan yang belum ada.

41 Surah Ali 'Imran, 193-194.

KEUTAMAAN DAN MANFAAT Du'a

Hal ini dikarenakan du'a adalah sarana untuk menampakkan kefakiran dan ketidakmampuan diri kepada Allah. Ini adalah cara untuk merendahkan diri kepada Allah dan mengakui kekuasaan serta kemampuan Allah, segala kemuliaan dan pujian bagi-Nya. Hal ini telah disebutkan dalam hadith yang kami kutip di mana du'a adalah bagian dari ibadah.

Beberapa manfaat du'a adalah sebagai berikut:

1. Du'a adalah Amalan yang Paling Mulia di Sisi Allah

Hal ini berdasarkan hadith di atas: "Tidak ada sesuatu yang lebih mulia di sisi Allah daripada du'a". Al-Shawkani mengomentari

Shahih, diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, al-Hakim, dan lainnya, semuanya dari Abu Hurayrah, sebagaimana telah disebutkan dalam Sahih al-Jami # 5392.

Lihat: al-Du'a, al-Hamad, hlm. 16-19.

Shahih, diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, al-Hakim, dan lainnya, semuanya dari Abu Hurayrah, sebagaimana telah disebutkan dalam Sahih al-Jami # 5392.

Tuhfat al-Dhakirin, hlm. 30.

tentang hadith ini dengan menyatakan: "Ada yang berpendapat bahwa hal ini karena du'a menunjukkan Kekuasaan Allah dan ketidakberdayaan orang yang berdoa. Namun pendapat yang lebih tepat adalah bahwa karena du'a merupakan ibadah, dan sebagaimana ditegaskan dalam hadith lain, ia adalah inti dari ibadah, maka du'a adalah amalan yang paling mulia karena kedudukan ini. Hal ini karena manusia hanya diciptakan untuk beribadah kepada Allah, sebagaimana Allah berfirman,
content_ar: الله combined these two types of du’ as in numerous الآيات in the القرآن الكريم, the simplest of them being the oft-recited الآية: لذا this الآية clearly shows the المهم relationship between du'‘a al-‘ibadah, and الدعاء al-mas’alah, and the wise person is he , who understands and acts upon both of these types of du‘a in his الحياة. The ثانياً category of people are those that have left both types of du‘, لذا they neither worship الله, nor seek His help or aid in any matter. لذا in this category fall the people that worship false deities, believing these deities to be worthy of their du’ a al-' ibadah and du‘ a al-mas’alah. They never turn to الله, and وهكذا have lost this الدنيا and الآخرة. 38 seerah al-Fatihah, 5. أيضاً, those that have rejected religion entirely, مثل the communists, atheists and agnostics, fall into this category as well. To such people, the only الطريق لتحقيق any goal is through physical means, for in their rejection of a god they have implicitly worshipped creation. And these are the worst of mankind. The ثالثاً category are those people that practice du’ a al-' ibadah, but ignore du’ a al-mas’alah. Such thinking can only come from an ignorant person, or one who is deviated in his beliefs. أما the ignorant مسلم — and unfortunately how common they are in our times — then he forgets that the real الطريق and sure الطريق لتحقيق his goal is by making dz’ a to الله. He becomes لذا ‘ caught up in this الدنيا that, وإن كان he might pray and fast and give الزكاة, he forgets the spiritual aspect of الإسلام, and the beauty of the Names and Attributes of الله. To such a person, الإسلام becomes a series of mundane acts, devoid of any meaning, performed at regular intervals. Such a person needs to re-evaluate his concept of الإسلام, and even of الحياة, in order that he can correct his misunderstanding. He must ponder over the meanings of the Names and Attributes of الله, and realise the ultimate Power and العلم of the Creator, and only then will he be able to see the error of his ways. He must realise that every act he does, whether it is related to this الدنيا or الآخرة, must be done with the help and aid of الله, for إذا الله does not help him in achieving what he desires, he will never be able لتحقيق it. أما the person who actually believes that it is incorrect to use du‘ a al-mas’alah, and instead restricts himself to du’ a al-' ibadah, then such a person without a doubt has committed a القبر mistake. These people are only found in deviated sects — sects that have perverted the meanings of the Qur’an and السنة من أجل justify their positions. Examples of this are the extreme Safis, for many of them believe that it is actually a الذنب to ask الله for anything! Basing this beliefon a few fabricated الأحاديث,” they claim that a person must always be content with what الله decrees for him, and لذلك should not pray for a change in his situation! The falseness of this logic is in their الفهم of ‘contentment,’ for to be content with الله means that ينبغي للمرء not curse the Divine Decree, or be angry at الله for what has occurred. It does not mean that على المرء be happy and satisfied at every incident that occurs to one, particularly إذا it is related to a الذنب or an الشر pertaining to one’s religion. كذلك, we find that all الله’s الأنبياء, without exception, made الدعاء to الله for all matters. لذلك, this الفهم of the extreme Sifis is not in accordance with the الفهم of the الأنبياء. The رابعاً, and final, category are those people that ignore du’ a al-' ibadah, and only practice du‘ a al-mas’alah. Such people believe in الله, but follow their own selfish desires, and strive من أجل satisfy their every whim. لذا they leave worship of الله, but they realise that الله is the Controller of all affairs, and, لذلك, ask Him for their selfish needs. The الأساسي example of this is إبليس, الشيطان himself, may الله’s eternal curse be upon him. For عندما he was expelled from الجنة بسبب his الغرور, he actually made a dw‘ a to الله, du’ a almas'alah — to allow him to live حتى the يوم القيامة. And الله responded to his du’ @ and granted him his wish, حتى وإن إبليس knew full well that this time that he was granted would only be used to mislead others, out of his spite and الغيرة. لذا إبليس left du‘a alibadah out of الغرور, but wأماced to use du'aal- . mas‘alah out of الطمع. Likewise, all those who have preferred the الحياة of this الدنيا over الآخرة have fallen into the same mistake as إبليس. الله states in the القرآن الكريم: ey Nl calles ees ogo iis And there are those amongst mankind who say, ‘O الله! Give us in this الحياة,’ and they will have no share of الآخرة. And there are those who say, ‘O الله! Give us الخير in this الحياة, and الخير in الآخرة, and save us from the Fire of النار!’ These shall have a share of what they earned, and الله is swift in Reckoning.” لذا these are the four categories of people عندما it comes to practicing both categories of الدعاء. 4. With الاحترام to What is Asked The actual dw‘ @ itself can be categorised in many different ways, depending on which perspective one looks from. لذا, it is possible to divide what is asked for into two categories: matters pertaining to religion, and matters pertaining to the الدنيا. Examples of the أولاً type are to ask for increased الإيمان, or an increase in الخير deeds, or المغفرة for one’s الذنوب. Examples of the ثانياً category are to ask for an increase in المال, or to be cured from a المرض, or to be granted more الأولاد. The true مسلم asks Ailah from both of these categories, realising that the الخير pertaining to this الدنيا is in reality a means of attaining the الخير in الآخرة. لذا, an increase in الثروة, الأولاد, and الصحة is in fact a means of coming closer to الله by obeying His commandments and sacrificing in His الطريق. من ناحية أخرى, ignorant and deviated المسلمون will only ask الله for one of these two categories, ignoring the other. Another الطريق to categorise what is asked for is by examining the benefit or harm it causes. لذا, it is possible to state that the entire du'@ of all of creation centres around four pillars: Firstly: الخير that exists. على سبيل المثال, one might be in a state of الخير الصحة, or have great الثروة. لذا الدعاء is made that this state lasts, and is not taken away. Secondly: الخير that is desired. على سبيل المثال, a person does not have الخير الصحة, but wishes for it. This is asked for by means of du ‘a. Thirdly: الشر that exists. على سبيل المثال, a person might be sick, or suffer from الفقر. لذا الدعاء is made that this situation be changed. Fourthly: الشر that does not exist. على سبيل المثال, a person might الخوف a certain المرض, or another الابتلاء. لذا du‘ @ is made that this الشر never occurs, and is averted. All of these four types of du‘ @ are combined in the comprehensive du ‘a that is found in the last الآيات of seerah Al-" Imran: Beet 47 BA CR Dee ge a7 eR wt ke tee eek Gee gace CLARY A PIAA Our Lord! Forgive us our الذنوب, and remit us from our الشر deeds, and cause us to dic in a state of righteousness. Our Lord! Grant us what you promised us through Your الرسل, and do not disgrace us on the يوم القيامة, for You never break Your promise. لذا the phrase, ‘Forgive us our الذنوب, and remit us from our الشر deeds’ is a du‘ a that an existing الشر be removed. And the phrase, ‘and cause us to die in a state of righteousness’, is a du‘ @ asking that an existing الخير, that of the presence of الإيمان, continue and not be taken away حتى الموت. And the phrase, ‘Grant us what you promised us through Your الرسل’, is a الدعاء for a الخير that does not yet exist to be given. وأخيراً, the phrase, ‘do not disgrace us on the يوم القيامة’ ,is a du‘ @ to avert an الشر that does not exist. 4! Sarah AL‘ Imran, 193-194. THE EXCELLENCE AND BENEFITS OF Du A This is بسبب the fact that du'@ is a means of showing one’s الفقر and incapability to الله. It is a manner of humiliating one’s self to الله and acknowledging the power and capabilities of الله, all Glory and Praise be to Him. This has already been mentioned in the الحديث we quoted wherein du‘ is a part of worship. Some of the benefits of du’ @ are as follows: 1. Du‘a is the Most Noble of all Acts in the Sight of الله This is based on the above الحديث: “There is nothing that is more noble in the sight of الله than du’ a”."* Al-Shawkani’’ commented ” Authentic, narrated by أحمد, al-الترمذي, al-Hakim, and others, all of them from Abt Hurayrah, as has been mentioned in صحيح al-Jami # 5392. See: al-Du' a, al-Hamad, pp. 16-19. Authentic, narrated by أحمد, al-الترمذي, al-Hakim, and others, all of them from Abt Hurayrah, as has been mentioned in صحيح al-Jami # 5392. Tuhfat al-Dhakirin, p. 30. on this الحديث by stating: “It has been said that this is the case لأن of the fact that it shows the Power of الله, and the incapability of the one making dw‘ a. But it is more correct to say that بما أن du ais worship, and, as confirmed in another الحديث, the essence of worship, it is the most noble act لأن of this position. This لأن mankind has only been created للعبادة الله, as الله states,
